specification.topic-statement: The agent session restore replay subsystem populates an agent's in-memory conversation history and scrollback display by iterating over the message records loaded from the database, converting each to its live representation and rendering each to the scrollback buffer.

scope.in-scope: Conversation population (filtering, conversion, token cache tracking), scrollback population (command and fork side-effect replay before each render), render error handling, and mark restoration from the replay context mark stack.
scope.boundary: How database records are queried and assembled into the replay context is handled by the agent restoration orchestrator and is out of scope here.
The logic that interprets command and fork event data to update agent configuration fields is handled by a separate command-effect replay subsystem and is out of scope here.
How individual message records are converted to scrollback display lines is handled by the event rendering subsystem and is out of scope here.

The structure of the token cache and how turns are defined within it are out of scope here; this subsystem only records that a new turn begins whenever a user-role message is encountered during replay.

field.name: messages
field.type: ordered array of database message records
field.note: chronologically ordered; includes all message kinds

field.name: count
field.note: number of entries in the messages array

field.name: mark stack
field.type: ordered collection of replay mark records
field.note: marks extracted during earlier phases of restoration

field.name: context index
field.note: the position within the conversation array that this mark references

field.name: label
field.note: optional human-readable label for the mark

field.name: message index
field.note: copied from the replay mark's context index

field.name: label
field.note: deep-copied from the replay mark's label; null if the source label is null

field.name: timestamp
field.note: always null after restore; not preserved from database

behavior.conversation-population:
The agent's live conversation array is built by iterating every entry in the replay context in order.
For each entry, two preconditions must both be satisfied for the message to be included in the conversation:
1. The message kind must be a conversation kind (one of: system, user, assistant, tool_call, tool_result, tool). 2. The message must not be marked as interrupted.
Messages that fail either precondition are silently skipped.
For each qualifying message, the database record is converted to an in-memory provider message object. If conversion fails, the error is logged and the message is skipped; restoration continues with the next entry without aborting.
System messages yield a null provider message object on successful conversion. A null result is not an error — system prompts are handled through the agent's system prompt field rather than the conversation array, so a null conversion result is silently ignored.
For all other qualifying messages that produce a non-null provider message object, the object is appended to the agent's live conversation array. If the message has user role, the token cache records a new turn at this point.
A message flagged as interrupted is not added to the live conversation even if its kind is a conversation kind. Interrupted messages represent incomplete LLM turns and must not be submitted to the provider as complete history.
If appending a successfully converted message to the conversation array fails (for example, due to memory exhaustion), the error is logged and the message is skipped. Restoration continues; the agent may start with a truncated conversation history.
behavior.scrollback-population:
The agent's scrollback display is reconstructed by iterating every entry in the replay context in order, including entries that were excluded from the conversation in the previous phase.
Before rendering each entry, command-side effects are applied if the message kind is "command" or "fork". This ensures that state changes recorded as historical commands (such as model selections or fork events that establish pinned documents and toolset filters) take effect before the corresponding event is rendered to the scrollback, keeping the displayed history consistent with the agent's configuration at each point in time.
After any applicable side-effects are applied, the entry is rendered to the scrollback buffer using the message kind, content, data, and interrupted flag. If rendering fails, the error is logged and the entry is skipped; scrollback reconstruction continues with the next entry without aborting.
Non-conversation kinds such as "command", "fork", and others are rendered to the scrollback even though they are excluded from the live conversation array. The scrollback is a complete historical display; the conversation array is a filtered input for the LLM.
Both "command" kind messages and "fork" kind messages are routed through the command-effect replay logic. Fork events carry pinned-document and toolset-filter data in their payload, which the replay logic extracts and applies to the agent.
behavior.marks-restoration:
If the replay context's mark stack contains one or more entries, an array of mark objects is allocated on the agent and populated in stack order.
For each entry in the mark stack a mark object is allocated and initialized with the entry's context index as the message index, a deep copy of the entry's label (or null if the label is absent), and a null timestamp. The mark is appended to the agent's mark array and the agent's mark count is incremented.
If the mark stack is empty, no mark array is allocated and the agent's mark count remains zero.
The marks restoration operation is defined but is excluded from coverage tracking and is not invoked by any current execution path in the agent restoration orchestrator. It exists as infrastructure for future use.
